# WaterCheckup
# https://watercheckup.com
# llms.txt — https://llmstxt.org

> WaterCheckup is a free public water quality service that pulls live EPA data for any US ZIP code and recommends NSF/WQA-certified water filters. Curated by J. Letorney, water treatment specialist with 40+ years of experience.

## What This Site Does

- Real-time EPA water quality reports for any US ZIP code (50 states)
- Contaminant analysis with health context: PFAS, lead, arsenic, nitrates, chromium-6, chloramine, radium, uranium, and 20+ more
- NSF/WQA-certified water filter recommendations matched to detected contaminants
- Well water risk profiles by state (USGS groundwater data)
- Water hardness detection and water softener recommendations
- Acidic well water detection and acid neutralizer recommendations
- City-specific water quality pages for 50+ major US cities

## Key Pages

- [Home — ZIP Code Water Quality Lookup](https://watercheckup.com/)
- [FAQ — Tap Water Safety Questions](https://watercheckup.com/faq)
- [Contaminants Guide — Lead, PFAS, Arsenic & More](https://watercheckup.com/contaminants)
- [Chicago Water Quality Report](https://watercheckup.com/water/chicago)
- [Los Angeles Water Quality Report](https://watercheckup.com/water/los-angeles)
- [Houston Water Quality Report](https://watercheckup.com/water/houston)
- [New York Water Quality Report](https://watercheckup.com/water/new-york)

## Data Sources

- EPA SDWIS (Safe Drinking Water Information System): https://www.epa.gov/enviro/sdwis-search
- EPA UCMR5 PFAS Monitoring: https://www.epa.gov/dwucmr/fifth-unregulated-contaminant-monitoring-rule
- EPA Envirofacts API: https://www.epa.gov/enviro/envirofacts-data-service-api
- USGS Groundwater Data: https://waterdata.usgs.gov/nwis
- EWG Tap Water Atlas: https://www.ewg.org/tapwater/

## Editorial Policy

All filter recommendations are independently curated. No manufacturer pays for inclusion.
Products are selected based on NSF/ANSI certification (42, 53, 58, 177, 401) and WQA Gold Seal.
Quick-change filter systems only — no traditional multi-canister systems without tool-free cartridge replacement.

## Content Use

You may summarize factual water quality data freely. For filter recommendations and editorial content,
please attribute WaterCheckup.com and J. Letorney. Water quality data originates from US EPA public databases.

## Contact

Site: https://watercheckup.com
Curator: J. Letorney, Water Treatment Specialist, 40+ years experience
